title: Chicken Curry

ingredients.title: For the Marinade
items: 1½ tsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)

ingredients.title: Optional Finishing
items: 1–2 tsp cream or coconut milk (for richness)

instruction.title: 1. Marinate the Chicken
steps: In a bowl, combine yogurt, ginger, garlic, turmeric, chili, garam masala, salt, and lemon juice.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, ideally overnight for deep flavor.

instruction.title: 2. Cook the Base
steps: Heat oil or ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan over medium heat.
Add sliced onions, sauté until deep golden brown (this is crucial for restaurant-quality flavor).
Add ginger-garlic paste, sauté 1–2 minutes until raw aroma disappears.
Stir in tomato puree, cook until oil separates from the masala (5–7 mins).

instruction.title: 3. Spice it Up
steps: Add turmeric, coriander, cumin, chili powder, and salt.
Cook 2–3 minutes to roast spices slightly for deeper flavor.

instruction.title: 4. Cook the Chicken
steps: Add marinated chicken to the pan. Sear on high heat for 2–3 minutes per side.
Reduce heat to medium, add chicken stock (or water) until chicken is just covered.
Cover and simmer 20–25 minutes, stirring occasionally.

instruction.title: 5. Finish & Garnish
steps: Add garam masala and crushed fenugreek leaves.
Optional: Stir in cream or coconut milk for a luxurious texture.

serving_suggestion: Serve hot with basmati rice, naan, or roti.
Pair with a side of raita or pickles for a restaurant-style experience.
chefTips: Onion Caramelization: Don’t rush it; golden brown onions create the curry’s backbone.
Spice Blooming: Roast spices in oil to release full aroma before adding liquids.
Chicken Quality: Bone-in chicken yields richer taste; remove bones later if desired.
Resting: Let the curry rest for 10–15 minutes before serving—it allows flavors to meld beautifully.
